summary: Fire‑charged Dark Star → St. Stephen → Eleven chain and monster Alligator jam
blurb: The show erupts with a blistering Dark Star → Saint Stephen → The Eleven sequence, followed by an all‑time great Alligator/Drums jam. Jerry’s soaring guitar tone and Pigpen’s organ drive the high‑energy flow, while Phil’s bass locks in tight on The Other One. Highlights include Lovelight’s fiery climax and a powerful Death Don’t Have No Mercy finish.
review: August 21, 1968 at Fillmore West captures the Dead at a creative peak. The first set rolls from a twisting Cryptical Envelopment → The Other One sandwich into a fierce Alligator/Drums excursion that showcases Bill’s propulsive drumming and Pigpen’s gritty organ. The second set is where the night truly shines: Dark Star launches into an unforgettable Saint Stephen → The Eleven suite, then barrels through Death Don’t Have No Mercy before closing with a soaring Turn On Your Lovelight. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s “crunchy, on‑fire” guitar, Phil’s “insane” bass dance with the lead, and Pigpen’s six‑note organ figure that lifts Dark Star into another dimension. Even with minor tape quirks (a cut ending on Lovelight), the soundboard captures the band’s raw power and seamless segues, making this a must‑listen snapshot of late‑’68 psychedelia.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across four of five recordings: repeated praise for Dark Star, Alligator, St. Stephen > Eleven sequence and Pigpen’s organ; no consistent negative comments. One mixed review mentions a minor diginoise but does not detract from overall enthusiasm.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard with balanced mix, minimal tape hiss and only minor edits; captures the full set and highlights without major dropouts.

key_highlights: Dark Star → Saint Stephen → The Eleven seamless sequence
Alligator/Drums jam that stitches the show together
Pigpen’s organ texture on Schoolgirl (Good Morning Little School Girl) and Alligator
Lovelight’s soaring climax despite a brief cut

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is described as crunchy, in‑your‑face and on fire; he leads the Dark Star, Alligator and Eleven jams with soaring lead lines and powerful vocals on Lovelight.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh delivers an insane bass foundation, weaving a psychic dance with Jerry during Dark Star and driving The Other One with relentless energy.
band_performance.Brent: Pigpen’s Hammond organ adds powerful texture throughout, highlighted by a memorable six‑note figure in Dark Star and thick organ swells on Good Morning Little School Girl and Alligator.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann propels the Alligator/Drums transition, provides a solid rhythmic backbone, and his snare intro on The Eleven is especially praised.
